rulings.Case Title: WILSHIRE COMMERCIAL CAPITAL LLC. DBA WILSHIRE VS. CHEE W KONG
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF WILSHIRE COMMERCIAL CAPITAL LLC. DBA WILSHIRE CONSUMER CREDIT's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The claim of exemption is granted in part. The sheriff shall withhold $300 per twice-monthly pay period or $600 per monthly pay period. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LELAND EVANS ET AL VS. DOMENIC J. CANNIZZARO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.S Verifications To Defendants July 14 And July 29 Amended Responses; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Attorney Of Record, Gregory De La Pena,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2025, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS LELAND EVANS, AND JUAN REYES' Motion To Compel Defendant Don Angelo Inc.'s Verifications To Defendants July 14 And July 29 Amended Responses; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Attorney Of Record, Gregory De La Pena. The unopposed motion to compel defendant Don Angelo Inc. to verify its amended responses to certain of plaintiff Leland Evans's special interrogatories, set two, is denied. The court has carefully reviewed the Wiederhold Declaration and it seems plain enough that the verification, which accompanied the July 29, 2025 amended responses, verifies the July 29 amended responses. That leaves the July 14, 2025 amended responses unverified, but upon comparison of the two sets of amended responses, the court finds that all of the factual information contained in the unverified July 14 responses is repeated in the verified July 29 responses. The court must award sanctions against a party who unsuccessfully makes a motion to compel further responses to interrogatories unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make sanctions unjust. (See Code Civ. Proc. 2029.300, subd. (d).) The court finds substantial justification and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: HONG ZHENG ET AL VS. ST. FRANCIS HOMES ASSOCIATION, A NON-PROFIT MUTUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hong Zheng And Hong Xiao Motion To Deem Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2025, Line 12. PLAINTIFF HONG ZHENG, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS TRUSTEE TO MACRO ROJO TRUST, AND HONG XIAO's Motion To Deem Admitted And For Sanctions. The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs Hong Zheng and Hong Xiao acknowledge that they received substantially compliant responses before the date of the hearing and they no longer seek a deemed admitted order. Sanctions are nonetheless warranted. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.280, subd. (c) ["It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion."].) The court orders St. Francis Homes Association to pay $3,905 to plaintiffs within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT SVES GO, LLC's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. rulings.Case Title: NURSES AND PROFESSIONAL HEALTHCARE VS. CLIPBOARD HEALTH LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Opposition To Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Exhibit B To The Declaration Of Michelle Alicante, And Declaration Of Wyatt Amaral And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2025, Line 10. DEFENDANTS CLIPBOARD HEALTH LLC, AND TWOMAGNETS, LLC's Motion To Seal Portions Of Opposition To Motion For Preliminary Injunction. The unopposed motion by defendants Clipboard Health LLC and TwoMagnets LLC for an order sealing its opposition papers to plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is granted. These defendants seek to seal portions of Exhibit B to the Declaration of Michelle Alicante in Support of the Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction and portions of the Declaration of Wyatt Amaral in Support of the Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction. The moving defendants have shown facts supporting their claim that the material sought to be sealed contains commercially sensitive information that they take steps to keep secret, and that denying this motion would likely result in competitive harm. The court finds based on facts in the Amaral Declaration submitted in support of this motion that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: IYO INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. DAN SARGENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application To File Documents Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Dylan W. Wiseman
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Wednesday, September 24, 2025, Line 11. 2 - PLAINTIFF IYO INC.'s Application To File Documents Under Seal. The unopposed motion by plaintiff Iyo Inc. for an order sealing the unredacted version of defendant Dan Sargent's notice of motion and special motion to strike pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 425.16, and the unredacted version of the declaration of Steven S. Kaufhold in support of the special motion to strike, is granted. Iyo has shown facts supporting its claim that the material sought to be sealed contains commercially sensitive information that Iyo takes steps to keep secret, and that denying this motion would likely result in competitive harm. The court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.
